verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is a type I single-pass TM protein that is predominantly ER-resident but translocates to ER-plasma membrane junctions upon ER Ca2+ depletion, where its cytoplasmic domain directly engages Orai1 channels in the PM. The protein body reaches the outer face via its own TM anchor in a documented, activation-dependent manner. This qualifies as dual localization with a regulated PM pool under store-depletion conditions.
